title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
hook: What if AI could learn to be harmless by following a written constitution?
promise: You will understand how Constitutional AI uses principles instead of human labels to align models.
prerequisite_note: Familiarity with how AI models are trained and why aligning their behavior is challenging is helpful.

glossary.plain: Ensuring an AI system's behavior matches human intentions and values.
A method where AI models are guided by a set of written rules or principles.
Training AI using ratings or corrections from humans to shape its responses.
The AI evaluates its own outputs against the constitution and adjusts accordingly.
The principle that an AI should avoid causing harm in its responses.
A document listing principles or rules that the AI should follow during training.

cards.title: AI by the Rules
body: See how written principles let AI judge itself, reducing the need for endless human feedback.

cards.title: Can AI follow rules like people do?
body: Imagine teaching a robot to be polite using a list of rules, not by correcting every mistake. Could this work for AI?
This paper explores how written principles, like a mini-constitution, can guide AI models to act safely and helpfully with less human oversight.

cards.title: From principles to safer AI
body: First, researchers write a constitution: a set of guidelines for the AI to follow. Instead of humans grading every answer, the AI uses these rules to judge its own outputs.
The model learns to self-critique, checking if its responses match the constitution. Over time, the AI improves its behavior, aiming for harmlessness and helpfulness.
The result is an AI that internalizes principles, relying less on human intervention, and more on clear, written values.
cards.caption: A constitution guides the AI, which then learns to judge and improve itself.

cards.title: Human feedback or AI self-judgment?
body: Toggle to see how training changes when AI uses written principles instead of constant human feedback.
cards.offNote: Requires many human ratings, which are slow, costly, and inconsistent.
cards.onNote: AI uses clear written rules to critique itself, reducing human effort and improving consistency.

cards.title: The trick: self-critiquing with principles
body: Instead of relying on endless human corrections, the AI is trained to compare its answers to a list of principles, like fairness or harmlessness.
The model learns not just to answer, but to reflect on its own outputs and revise them to better match the written constitution.

cards.title: How much should we trust AI to judge itself?
body: Slide to see what happens as we rely more on AI self-critique versus human oversight.
cards.low: Every response is checked by a human. Reliable but slow and expensive.
cards.mid: AI critiques most responses, with some human spot-checking. Faster, but occasional errors slip through.
cards.high: AI fully judges itself using the constitution. Scalable, but risks missing subtle harms humans would catch.

cards.title: Choose the right ingredients for Constitutional AI
body: Pick the components needed to train an AI that follows written rules.

choices.label: Written constitution
choices.note: Provides the guiding principles for behavior.

choices.label: Self-critique mechanism
choices.note: Lets the AI review and improve its own answers.

choices.label: Initial human feedback
choices.note: Kickstarts the training process and sets the direction.

choices.label: Reward model
choices.note: Measures how well the AI follows the constitution.

choices.label: Reinforcement learning
choices.note: Optimizes the AI's responses based on feedback.

choices.label: Red-teaming
choices.note: Tests the AI with challenging prompts to find weaknesses.

cards.title: Constitutional AI in practice: safer and scalable
body: Constitutional AI produced models that were more harmless and helpful, with less need for human raters.
The approach made AI training more scalable, while still improving on key alignment metrics compared to traditional RLHF.
cards.caption: The model became safer while reducing the need for costly human oversight.

cards.title: Why Constitutional AI matters
body: This paper showed that AI can learn to follow rules, not just mimic human ratings, making alignment more scalable.
By shifting the focus from endless human correction to clear principles, Constitutional AI points toward safer, more autonomous systems.

recap.label: Write
recap.note: Draft a clear constitution of principles for the AI.

recap.label: Critique
recap.note: Train the AI to judge its own answers by these rules.

recap.label: Reinforce
recap.note: Use rewards to shape better, more aligned responses.

recap.label: Scale
recap.note: Reduce human labor while improving safety and helpfulness.

cards.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
body: Read the original work for technical details and experimental results.
